skill.name: domain-name-brainstormer
skill.description: Generates domain name ideas for projects, products, brands, and personal sites, with guidance on TLD fit, naming quality, and availability checks.
skill.summary: Brainstorm brandable domain names and compare practical TLD options.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found only Markdown examples and ordinary naming guidance. No executable code, scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration behavior was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution locations are fenced Markdown prompt examples and sample output. They are not executable Ruby code and do not instruct the assistant to run shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown only, and the flagged regions are code fences containing natural language prompts or example output. No shell metacharacter usage or command invocation appears in those locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts match ordinary prose, TLD names, or domain examples. No hashing, encryption, signing, or cryptographic API usage appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines discuss domain naming, design, availability, or workflow steps. No evidence of cryptographic implementation or unsafe algorithm selection was found.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts occur in naming tips about pronounceability and avoiding numbers. No host, user, network, process, or environment discovery behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact contexts are user-facing naming advice, not commands or instructions to inspect a system. No reconnaissance intent or capability is evident.

content.value_statement: Finding a memorable available domain can slow down a launch or naming decision. This skill generates focused name options and helps compare TLD fit, brand value, and availability signals.
actual_capabilities: Generates creative domain name options from a project, product, or brand description.
Compares common TLDs such as .com, .io, .dev, .ai, .app, .co, and .tech.
Explains why suggested names may be memorable, descriptive, or brandable.
Suggests alternatives when preferred names are taken or too expensive.
Provides workflow guidance for startup, product, and personal brand naming.
Reminds users to check social handles, trademarks, and long-term brand fit.
limitations: Availability and pricing can change quickly and must be verified with a registrar.
Trademark guidance is not legal advice and needs professional review.
The skill does not include executable registrar, WHOIS, or social media integrations.
Name quality depends on the clarity of the project and audience description.

use_cases.title: Name a Startup
use_cases.description: Generate short domain options for a new company and compare .com, .io, and .ai choices.

use_cases.title: Launch a Product
use_cases.description: Create brandable product names and rank them by clarity, memorability, and TLD fit.

use_cases.title: Build a Personal Brand
use_cases.description: Find domain variations for a portfolio, creator site, or professional profile.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Domain Ideas
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a personal finance app for freelancers. Suggest domain names and explain the strongest options.

prompt_templates.title: TLD-Focused Shortlist
prompt_templates.prompt: Brainstorm short domain names for an AI writing assistant. Focus on .ai, .io, and .com options.

prompt_templates.title: Keyword-Constrained Naming
prompt_templates.prompt: Suggest domain names for my design studio using the words pixel, studio, or canvas. Include brandability notes.

prompt_templates.title: Competitive Pattern Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Review naming patterns used by project management tools, then suggest available-sounding domain ideas for a remote team planning app.

output_examples.input: Suggest domains for a developer tool that helps teams share code snippets.
output: snippet.dev as a developer-focused top pick with a clear TLD match.
codeclip.com as a short, descriptive option with broad recognition.
sharecode.app as a direct product-style name for a web application.

output_examples.input: I need name ideas for a sustainable fashion marketplace.
output: A ranked shortlist across .com, .eco, and .fashion options.
Notes on pronunciation, spelling, and whether each name sounds premium or accessible.
Follow-up checks for trademarks and social handle consistency.

best_practices: Describe the target audience, product category, tone, and preferred TLDs before asking for names.
Verify final domain availability, pricing, trademarks, and social handles before making a purchase.
Ask for a ranked shortlist with reasons instead of accepting a long unfiltered list.
anti_patterns: Do not assume a suggested domain is available without checking a registrar.
Do not choose names that are hard to spell, pronounce, or share verbally.
Do not skip trademark review when the name will represent a business or product.

faq.question: Can this skill guarantee domain availability?
faq.answer: No. It can guide availability checks, but final status must be verified with a registrar.

faq.question: Which TLDs does it consider?
faq.answer: It discusses common options such as .com, .io, .dev, .ai, .app, .co, .xyz, .design, and .tech.

faq.question: Can it help with premium domains?
faq.answer: It can flag that taken or premium domains may cost more, but it cannot guarantee current market prices.

faq.question: Is it useful for personal brands?
faq.answer: Yes. It can suggest name-based domains, portfolio domains, and professional profile options.

faq.question: Does it check trademarks?
faq.answer: It reminds users to check trademarks, but it does not provide legal advice.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the project purpose, audience, desired tone, keywords, preferred TLDs, and any names to avoid.
